revision.comment: 仙台市、広瀬川の畔にある横穴墓群
revision.text:
'''宗禅寺横穴墓群'''（そうぜんじよこあなぼぐん）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]根岸町にある[[横穴墓]]群である。'''宗禅寺横穴群'''とも呼ばれる。7世紀後半から8世紀後半に、[[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]の右岸（南西岸）に作られた。総数は不明だが、発見された穴は15ある。
==立地== [[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]右岸で丘陵から平地に変わる一帯には、[[愛宕山横穴墓群]]、[[大年寺山横穴墓群]]などがあり、宗禅寺横穴墓群とともに[[向山横穴墓群]]をなす。宗禅寺横穴墓群は、[[大年寺山]]の尾根の先を広瀬川がかすめる位置、[[宗禅寺]]の墓地にある。現在は山裾と広瀬川の河原の間に川から10メートルほどの高さの平らな段丘があるが、古代には大年寺山の尾根がもう少し伸びて小さな谷をなしていた。
その谷の傾斜を横から堀りこんで墓としたのが、宗禅寺横穴墓群である。東西に並ぶようにして知られる限り15ある。後に谷は土砂で埋もれ、2、3メートルほどの崖として残り、横穴も岸から離れた一部を除いて地面の下になった。発掘調査は埋没谷の深部まで掘り下げずに終えたので、下のほうにまだ知られていない横穴が存在する可能性がある。
==横穴墓の概要== 発掘担当者によって、15の横穴墓は西から東へ、山に近いほうから川に近いほうへ、1号墳から15号墳と番号を振られた。1953年に見つかった穴が15号墳である。墓は崖面に直角にまっすぐ入り、崖の形にそって、西の端では南を、東の端では東南を向く。7、8、9号墳を除けばほぼ同じ高さにそろう。
横穴の奥に玄室があり、方形・長方形・台形で、床の1辺は1.4メートルから2.75メートルにおさまる。玄室の上はアーチ状であったり家の屋根の形であったりする。入り口（玄門）は、そこに至るトンネル（羨道）より幅を狭く作る。13号墳だけは、幅0.8メートルで、玄室・玄門・羨道の区別がないただのトンネルと見える。11号墳と12号墳は玄室が接触して数十センチの穴が通じていた。1号墳、2号墳、3号墳も玄室が横でつながっていたが、これはピッケルのような工具で穴を開けたらしく、後世の改変と考えられる。
7つの横穴では、約10センチメートルほど高くした台床が作られた。そのうち15号墳だけは高さが約50センチメートルで、顕著な壇をなした。台床がない穴は8である。岩には水が滲み出る自然の割れ目が走っていたため、横穴の中にはこれにあわせて排水用の溝を設けたものがある。台床の上で、5号墳から1体分、9号墳から3体分にあたると推測される人骨が見つかった。泥質化が著しく、見つかったのは頭蓋骨など大きな骨が少数で、状態はよくなかった。5号墳のものは熟練男性、9号墳のものは熟練男性2人と壮年女性1人と推定された。
遺物としては、7つの横穴から[[須恵器]]13点、[[土師器]]8点、鉄製刀子1点が見つかった。須恵器・土師器の発見場所は、玄室内、玄門閉塞部の外、穴の外の前庭部の3箇所である。それぞれ、遺体とともに副葬したもの、葬儀の後に供献したもの、追葬のために準備して置いたものに対応すると考えられる。刀子は約15.6センチで、木の柄がついており、5号墳の台床の上、つまり人骨と同じ場所にあった。
==その後の歴史== 横穴の利用が絶えて久しくなった中世に、この低い崖から少し北に離れて[[宗禅寺]]が開山した。遺跡がある崖は宗禅寺の所有地となり、崖上と崖下が両方とも寺の墓地になった。4号墳では、近世以後に作られた墓穴の床が横穴の天井に接近して作られ、崩れて30センチほどの穴が発掘調査時に開いていた。
穴の存在は寺にも近所の人にも知られており、浮浪者が住みついたこともあった。時代が不明だがおそらく居住者が拡張のために3つの横穴を内部で連結した。昭和の初めに、安全のためなどの理由で、付近の住民が地表に出てくる穴の入り口を土で埋めて閉塞した。
[[1953年]]、[[宮沢橋]]架け替えに伴い広瀬川に沿う道路を造る工事の際、削った崖の中から横穴が一つ見つかった。1970年代に、もう一段岸から離れたところに[[広瀬河畔通り]]を通すことになり、宗禅寺の墓地の移転が必要になった。横穴があった崖が移転先に含まれることとなり、仙台市教育委員会と仙台市建設局土木部が[[1975年]]1月13日から2月15日にかけて約300m<sup>2</sup>の発掘調査を実施した。既知のものと併せて14の横穴を確認、調査した。調査後、整地のために一部が破壊され、大部分が土中に埋められた。遺跡は現在は宗禅寺の墓地の下にあり、横穴の存在をうかがわせるものは何もない。
==参考文献== *仙台市教育委員会「仙台市根岸町宗禅寺横穴群発掘調査報告書」（仙台市文化財調査報告書第9集）、1976年3月。
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうせんしよこあなくん}} [[Category:宮城県の古墳]]

revision.comment: 郡山遺跡と南小泉遺跡
revision.text:
'''宗禅寺横穴墓群'''（そうぜんじよこあなぼぐん）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]根岸町にある[[横穴墓]]群である。'''宗禅寺横穴群'''とも呼ばれる。7世紀後半から8世紀後半に、[[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]の右岸（南西岸）に作られた。総数は不明だが、発見された穴は15ある。
==立地== [[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]右岸で丘陵から平地に変わる一帯には、[[愛宕山横穴墓群]]、[[大年寺山横穴墓群]]などがあり、宗禅寺横穴墓群とともに[[向山横穴墓群]]をなす。宗禅寺横穴墓群は、[[大年寺山]]の尾根の先を広瀬川がかすめる位置、[[宗禅寺]]の墓地にある。現在は山裾と広瀬川の河原の間に川から10メートルほどの高さの平らな段丘があるが、古代には大年寺山の尾根がもう少し伸びて小さな谷をなしていた。
その谷の傾斜を横から堀りこんで墓としたのが、宗禅寺横穴墓群である。東西に並ぶようにして知られる限り15ある。後に谷は土砂で埋もれ、2、3メートルほどの崖として残り、横穴も岸から離れた一部を除いて地面の下になった。発掘調査は埋没谷の深部まで掘り下げずに終えたので、下のほうにまだ知られていない横穴が存在する可能性がある。
遺跡の南東には[[724年]]まで[[陸奥国]]の[[国府]]があったとされる[[郡山遺跡]]があり、時期的に横穴墓の使用期間と重なる。広瀬川の対岸、東方には古墳時代から続く集落である[[南小泉遺跡]]がある。これらが横穴墓の利用者の居住地の候補である。

7つの横穴では、約10センチメートルほど高くした台床が作られた。そのうち15号墳だけは高さが約50センチメートルで、顕著な壇をなした。台床がない穴は8である。岩には水が滲み出る自然の割れ目が走っていたため、横穴の中にはこれにあわせて排水用の溝を設けたものがある。台床の上で、5号墳から1体分、9号墳から3体分にあたると推測される人骨が見つかった。泥質化が著しく、見つかったのは頭蓋骨など大きな骨が少数で、状態はよくなかった。5号墳のものは熟練男性、9号墳のものは熟練男性2人と壮年女性1人と推定された。
遺物としては、7つの横穴から[[須恵器]]13点、[[土師器]]8点、鉄製刀子1点が見つかった。須恵器・土師器の発見場所は、玄室内、玄門閉塞部の外、穴の外の前庭部の3箇所である。それぞれ、遺体とともに副葬したもの、葬儀の後に供献したもの、追葬のために準備して置いたものに対応すると考えられる。刀子は約15.6センチで、木の柄がついており、5号墳の台床の上、つまり人骨と同じ場所にあった。
==その後の歴史== 横穴の利用が絶えて久しくなった中世に、この低い崖から少し北に離れて[[宗禅寺]]が開山した。遺跡がある崖は宗禅寺の所有地となり、崖上と崖下が両方とも寺の墓地になった。4号墳では、近世以後に作られた墓穴の床が横穴の天井に接近して作られ、崩れて30センチほどの穴が発掘調査時に開いていた。

[[1953年]]、[[宮沢橋]]架け替えに伴い広瀬川に沿う道路を造る工事の際、削った崖の中から横穴が一つ見つかった。1970年代に、もう一段岸から離れたところに[[広瀬河畔通り]]を通すことになり、宗禅寺の墓地の移転が必要になった。横穴があった崖が移転先に含まれることとなり、仙台市教育委員会と仙台市建設局土木部が[[1975年]]1月13日から2月15日にかけて約300m<sup>2</sup>の発掘調査を実施した。既知のものと併せて14の横穴を確認、調査した。調査後、整地のために一部が破壊され、大部分が土中に埋められた。遺跡は現在は宗禅寺の墓地の下にあり、横穴の存在をうかがわせるものは何もない。

'''宗禅寺横穴墓群'''（そうぜんじよこあなぼぐん）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]根岸町にある[[横穴墓]]群である。'''宗禅寺横穴群'''とも呼ばれる。7世紀後半から8世紀後半に、[[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]の右岸（南西岸）に作られた。総数は不明だが、発見された穴は15ある。
==立地== [[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]右岸で丘陵から平地に変わる一帯には、[[愛宕山横穴墓群]]、[[大年寺山横穴墓群]]などがあり、宗禅寺横穴墓群とともに[[向山横穴墓群]]をなす。宗禅寺横穴墓群は、[[大年寺山]]の尾根の先を広瀬川がかすめる位置、[[宗禅寺]]の墓地にある。現在は山裾と広瀬川の河原の間に川から10メートルほどの高さの平らな段丘があるが、古代には大年寺山の尾根がもう少し伸びて小さな谷をなしていた。
その谷の傾斜を横から堀りこんで墓としたのが、宗禅寺横穴墓群である。東西に並ぶようにして知られる限り15ある。後に谷は土砂で埋もれ、2、3メートルほどの崖として残り、横穴も岸から離れた一部を除いて地面の下になった。発掘調査は埋没谷の深部まで掘り下げずに終えたので、下のほうにまだ知られていない横穴が存在する可能性がある。
遺跡の南東には[[724年]]まで[[陸奥国]]の[[国府]]があったとされる[[郡山遺跡]]があり、時期的に横穴墓の使用期間と重なる。広瀬川の対岸、東方には古墳時代から続く集落である[[南小泉遺跡]]がある。これらが横穴墓の利用者の居住地の候補である。
==横穴墓の概要== 発掘担当者によって、15の横穴墓は西から東へ、山に近いほうから川に近いほうへ、1号墳から15号墳と番号を振られた。1953年に見つかった穴が15号墳である。墓は崖面に直角にまっすぐ入り、崖の形にそって、西の端では南を、東の端では東南を向く。7、8、9号墳を除けばほぼ同じ高さにそろう。
横穴の奥に玄室があり、方形・長方形・台形で、床の1辺は1.4メートルから2.75メートルにおさまる。玄室の上はアーチ状であったり家の屋根の形であったりする。入り口（玄門）は、そこに至るトンネル（羨道）より幅を狭く作る。13号墳だけは、幅0.8メートルで、玄室・玄門・羨道の区別がないただのトンネルと見える。11号墳と12号墳は玄室が接触して数十センチの穴が通じていた。1号墳、2号墳、3号墳も玄室が横でつながっていたが、これはピッケルのような工具で穴を開けたらしく、後世の改変と考えられる。
7つの横穴では、約10センチメートルほど高くした台床が作られた。そのうち15号墳だけは高さが約50センチメートルで、顕著な壇をなした。台床がない穴は8である。岩には水が滲み出る自然の割れ目が走っていたため、横穴の中にはこれにあわせて排水用の溝を設けたものがある。台床の上で、5号墳から1体分、9号墳から3体分にあたると推測される人骨が見つかった。泥質化が著しく、見つかったのは頭蓋骨など大きな骨が少数で、状態はよくなかった。5号墳のものは熟練男性、9号墳のものは熟練男性2人と壮年女性1人と推定された。
遺物としては、7つの横穴から[[須恵器]]13点、[[土師器]]8点、鉄製刀子1点が見つかった。須恵器・土師器の発見場所は、玄室内、玄門閉塞部の外、穴の外の前庭部の3箇所である。それぞれ、遺体とともに副葬したもの、葬儀の後に供献したもの、追葬のために準備して置いたものに対応すると考えられる。刀子は約15.6センチで、木の柄がついており、5号墳の台床の上、つまり人骨と同じ場所にあった。
==その後の歴史== 横穴の利用が絶えて久しくなった中世に、この低い崖から少し北に離れて[[宗禅寺]]が開山した。遺跡がある崖は宗禅寺の所有地となり、崖上と崖下が両方とも寺の墓地になった。4号墳では、近世以後に作られた墓穴の床が横穴の天井に接近して作られ、崩れて30センチほどの穴が発掘調査時に開いていた。
穴の存在は寺にも近所の人にも知られており、浮浪者が住みついたこともあった。時代が不明だがおそらく居住者が拡張のために3つの横穴を内部で連結した。昭和の初めに、安全のためなどの理由で、付近の住民が地表に出てくる穴の入り口を土で埋めて閉塞した。
[[1953年]]、[[宮沢橋 (広瀬川)|宮沢橋]]架け替えに伴い広瀬川に沿う道路を造る工事の際、削った崖の中から横穴が一つ見つかった。1970年代に、もう一段岸から離れたところに[[広瀬河畔通り]]を通すことになり、宗禅寺の墓地の移転が必要になった。横穴があった崖が移転先に含まれることとなり、仙台市教育委員会と仙台市建設局土木部が[[1975年]]1月13日から2月15日にかけて約300m<sup>2</sup>の発掘調査を実施した。既知のものと併せて14の横穴を確認、調査した。調査後、整地のために一部が破壊され、大部分が土中に埋められた。遺跡は現在は宗禅寺の墓地の下にあり、横穴の存在をうかがわせるものは何もない。
==参考文献== *仙台市教育委員会「仙台市根岸町宗禅寺横穴群発掘調査報告書」（仙台市文化財調査報告書第9集）、1976年3月。
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうせんしよこあなくん}} [[Category:宮城県の古墳]] [[Category:群集墳]]

==立地== [[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]右岸で丘陵から平地に変わる一帯には、[[愛宕山横穴墓群]]、[[大年寺山横穴墓群]]などがあり、宗禅寺横穴墓群とともに[[向山横穴墓群]]をなす。宗禅寺横穴墓群は、[[大年寺山]]の尾根の先を広瀬川がかすめる位置、[[宗禅寺]]の墓地にある。現在は山裾と広瀬川の河原の間に川から比高10メートルほどの平らな[[段丘]]があるが、古代には大年寺山の[[尾根]]がもう少し伸びて小さな谷をなしていた。
その谷の傾斜を横から掘りこんで墓としたのが、宗禅寺横穴墓群である。東西に並ぶようにして知られる限り[[横穴墓]]は15基確認されている。後に谷は[[土砂]]で埋もれ、2、3メートルほどの崖として残り、横穴も岸から離れた一部を除いて地面の下になった。[[発掘調査]]は埋没谷の深部まで掘り下げずに終えたので、下のほうにまだ知られていない横穴が存在する可能性がある。
遺跡の南東には[[724年]]まで[[陸奥国]]の[[国府]]があったとされる[[郡山遺跡]]があり、時期的に横穴墓の使用期間と重なる。広瀬川の対岸、東方には[[古墳時代]]から続く集落である[[南小泉遺跡]]がある。これらが横穴墓の利用者の居住地の候補である。
==横穴墓の概要== 発掘担当者によって、15の横穴墓は西から東へ、山に近いほうから川に近いほうへ、1号墳から15号墳と番号を振られた。[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に見つかった穴が15号墳である。墓は崖面に直角にまっすぐ入り、崖の形にそって、西の端では南を、東の端では東南を向く。7、8、9号墳を除けばほぼ同じ高さにそろう。
横穴の奥に[[玄室]]があり、断面形状は方形・[[長方形]]・[[台形]]で、床の1辺は1.4メートルから2.75メートルにおさまる。玄室の上は[[アーチ]]状であったり家の屋根の形であったりする。入り口（玄門）は、そこに至るトンネル（[[羨道]]）より幅を狭く作る。13号墳だけは、幅0.8メートルで、玄室・玄門・羨道の区別がないタイプである。11号墳と12号墳は玄室が接触して数十センチの穴が通じていた。1号墳、2号墳、3号墳も玄室が横でつながっていたが、これは[[ピッケル]]のような[[工具]]で穴を開けたらしく、後世の改変と考えられる。
7つの横穴では、約10センチメートルほど高くした台床が作られた。そのうち15号墳だけは高さが約50センチメートルで、顕著な壇をなした。台床がない穴は8である。岩には水が滲み出る自然の割れ目が走っていたため、横穴の中にはこれにあわせて排水用の溝を設けたものがある。台床の上で、5号墳から1体分、9号墳から3体分にあたると推測される人骨が見つかった。泥質化が著しく、見つかったのは頭蓋骨など大きな骨が少数で、状態はよくなかった。5号墳のものは熟練男性、9号墳のものは熟練男性2人と壮年女性1人と推定された。
遺物としては、7つの横穴から[[須恵器]]13点、[[土師器]]8点、鉄製[[刀子]]1点が見つかった。須恵器・土師器の発見場所は、玄室内、玄門閉塞部の外、穴の外の前庭部の3箇所である。それぞれ、遺体とともに副葬したもの、葬儀の後に供献したもの、追葬のために準備して置いたものに対応すると考えられる。刀子は約15.6センチで、木の柄がついており、5号墳の台床の上、つまり人骨と同じ場所にあった。
==その後の歴史== 横穴の利用が絶えて久しくなった[[中世]]に、この低い崖から少し北に離れて[[宗禅寺]]が開山した。遺跡がある崖は宗禅寺の所有地となり、崖上と崖下が両方とも寺の墓地になった。4号墳では、近世以後に作られた墓穴の床が横穴の天井に接近して作られ、崩れて30センチほどの穴が発掘調査時に開いていた。
穴の存在は寺にも近所の人にも知られており、浮浪者が住みついたこともあった。時代が不明だがおそらく居住者が拡張のために3つの横穴を内部で連結した。昭和の初めに、安全のためなどの理由で、付近の住民が地表に出てくる穴の入り口を土で埋めて閉塞した。
[[1953年]]、[[宮沢橋 (広瀬川)|宮沢橋]]架け替えに伴い広瀬川に沿う道路を造る工事の際、削った崖の中から横穴が一つ見つかった。[[1970年代]]に、もう一段岸から離れたところに[[広瀬河畔通り]]を通すことになり、宗禅寺の墓地の移転が必要になった。横穴があった崖が移転先に含まれることとなり、仙台市教育委員会と仙台市建設局土木部が[[1975年]]（昭和50年）1月13日から2月15日にかけて約300m<sup>2</sup>の発掘調査を実施した。既知のものと併せて14の横穴を確認、調査した。調査後、[[整地]]のために一部が破壊され、大部分が土中に埋められた。遺跡は現在は宗禅寺の墓地の下にあり、横穴の存在をうかがわせるものは何もない。
==参考文献== *仙台市教育委員会「仙台市根岸町宗禅寺横穴群発掘調査報告書」（仙台市文化財調査報告書第9集）、1976年3月。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/Category/bunka/02.html 仙台市の文化財]（[[仙台市教育委員会]]） ** [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000065.html 宗禅寺横穴墓群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうせんしよこあなくん}} [[Category:宮城県の古墳]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.comment: 推敲
revision.text:
'''宗禅寺横穴墓群'''（そうぜんじよこあなぼぐん）は、[[宮城県]][[仙台市]][[太白区]]根岸町にある[[横穴墓]]群である。'''宗禅寺横穴群'''とも呼ばれる。7世紀後半から8世紀後半に、[[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]の右岸（南西岸）に作られた。総数は不明だが、発見された穴は15ある。
==立地== [[広瀬川 (宮城県)|広瀬川]]右岸で丘陵から平地に変わる一帯には、[[愛宕山横穴墓群]]、[[大年寺山横穴墓群]]などがあり、宗禅寺横穴墓群とともに[[向山横穴墓群]]をなす。宗禅寺横穴墓群は、[[大年寺山]]の尾根の先を広瀬川がかすめる位置、[[宗禅寺]]の墓地にある。現在は山裾と広瀬川の河原の間に川から比高10メートルほどの平らな[[段丘]]があるが、古代には大年寺山の[[尾根]]がもう少し伸びて小さな谷をなしていた。
その谷の傾斜を横から掘りこんで墓としたのが、宗禅寺横穴墓群である。東西に並ぶようにして知られる限り[[横穴墓]]は15基確認されている。後に谷は[[土砂]]で埋もれ、2、3メートルほどの崖として残り、横穴も岸から離れた一部を除いて地面の下になった。[[発掘調査]]は埋没谷の深部まで掘り下げずに終えたので、下のほうにまだ知られていない横穴が存在する可能性がある<ref name=hokokusho8p>『仙台市根岸町宗禅寺横穴群発掘調査報告書』8頁。</ref>。
遺跡の南東には[[724年]]まで[[陸奥国]]の[[国府]]があったとされる[[郡山遺跡]]があり、時期的に横穴墓の使用期間と重なる。広瀬川の対岸、東方には[[古墳時代]]から続く集落である[[南小泉遺跡]]がある。これらが横穴墓の利用者の居住地の候補である。
==横穴墓の概要== 発掘担当者によって、15の横穴墓は西から東へ、山に近いほうから川に近いほうへ、1号墳から15号墳と番号を振られた。[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に見つかった穴が15号墳である。どの墓も崖面に対して直角にまっすぐ入り、崖がのびる方向に従い、西の端では南を、東の端では東南を向く。7、8、9号墳を除けばほぼ同じ高さにそろう。
横穴の奥に[[玄室]]があり、断面形状は方形・[[長方形]]・[[台形]]で、床の1辺は1.4メートルから2.75メートルにおさまる。玄室の上は[[アーチ]]状であったり家の屋根の形であったりする。入り口（玄門）は、そこに至るトンネル（[[羨道]]）より幅を狭く作る。13号墳だけは、幅0.8メートルで、玄室・玄門・羨道の区別がないタイプである。11号墳と12号墳は玄室が接触して数十センチの穴が通じていた。1号墳、2号墳、3号墳も玄室が横でつながっていたが、これは[[ピッケル]]のような[[工具]]で穴を開けたらしく、後世の改変と考えられる。
7つの横穴では、約10センチメートルほど高くした台床が作られた。そのうち15号墳だけは高さが約50センチメートルで、顕著な壇をなした。台床がない穴は8である。岩には水が滲み出る自然の割れ目が走っていたため、横穴の中にはこれにあわせて排水用の溝を設けたものがある。台床の上で、5号墳から1体分、9号墳から3体分にあたると推測される人骨が見つかった。泥質化が著しく、見つかったのは頭蓋骨など大きな骨が少数で、状態はよくなかった。5号墳のものは熟年男性、9号墳のものは熟年男性2人と壮年女性1人と推定された。
遺物としては、7つの横穴から[[須恵器]]13点、[[土師器]]8点、鉄製[[刀子]]1点が見つかった。須恵器・土師器の発見場所は、玄室内、玄門閉塞部の外、穴の外の前庭部の3箇所である。それぞれ、遺体とともに副葬したもの、葬儀の後に供献したもの、追葬のために準備して置いたものに対応すると考えられる。刀子は約15.6センチで、木の柄がついており、5号墳の台床の上、つまり人骨と同じ場所にあった。
==その後の歴史== 横穴の利用が絶えて久しくなった中世に、この低い崖から少し北に離れて[[宗禅寺 (仙台市)|宗禅寺]]が開山した。遺跡がある崖は宗禅寺の所有地となり、崖上と崖下が両方とも寺の墓地になった<ref name=hokokusho4p>『仙台市根岸町宗禅寺横穴群発掘調査報告書』4頁。</ref>。4号墳では、近世以後に作られた墓穴の床が横穴の天井に接近して作られ、崩れて30センチほどの穴が発掘調査時に開いていた<ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>。
穴の存在は寺にも近所の人にも知られており、浮浪者が住みついたこともあった。時代が不明だがおそらく居住者が拡張のために3つの横穴を内部で連結した。昭和の初めに、安全のためなどの理由で、付近の住民が地表に出てくる穴の入り口を土で埋めて閉塞した<ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>。
[[1953年]]、[[宮沢橋 (広瀬川)|宮沢橋]]架け替えに伴い広瀬川に沿う道路を造る工事の際、削った崖の中から横穴が一つ見つかった<ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>。[[1970年代]]に、もう一段岸から離れたところに[[広瀬河畔通り]]を通すことになり、宗禅寺の墓地の移転が必要になった。横穴があった崖が移転先に含まれることとなり、仙台市教育委員会と仙台市建設局土木部が[[1975年]]（昭和50年）1月13日から2月15日にかけて約300m<sup>2</sup>の発掘調査を実施した。既知のものと併せて14の横穴を確認、調査した。調査後、[[整地]]のために一部が破壊され、大部分が土中に埋められた。遺跡は現在は宗禅寺の墓地の下にあり、横穴の存在をうかがわせるものは何もない。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *仙台市教育委員会『仙台市根岸町宗禅寺横穴群発掘調査報告書』（仙台市文化財調査報告書第9集）、1976年3月。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.sendai.jp/Category/bunka/02.html 仙台市の文化財]（[[仙台市教育委員会]]） ** [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000065.html 宗禅寺横穴墓群]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうせんしよこあなくん}} [[Category:宮城県の古墳]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombsnger extant == * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}[[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.
== References == {{reflist}}

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.

revision.text:
'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Koriyama Site|ja|郡山遺跡}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]]

'''Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group''' (宗禅寺横穴墓群) is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Negishimachi, Taihaku Ward, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture]]. It is also called '''Sōzenji Yokouana Group'''. These tombs were constructed on the right (southwest) bank of the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]] from the late 7th century to the late 8th century. While the total number of tombs is unknown, 15 have been discovered.
== Location == The area where the [[Hirose River (Miyagi)|Hirose River]]'s right bank transitions from hills to flatland contains other tomb groups, such as the {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} and {{ill|Dainenji-san Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群}}. Along with the Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group, these form the {{ill|Mukaiyama Yokouana Tomb Group|ja|向山横穴墓群}}. The Sōzenji Yokouana Tomb Group is situated where the ridge of {{ill|Dainenji-san|ja|大年寺山}} extends and nearly touches the Hirose River, within the cemetery of {{ill|Sōzenji|ja|宗禅寺}}. Today, there is a flat [[terrace (geology)|terrace]] about 10 meters above the river, but in ancient times, the ridge extended further, forming a small valley.
The tombs of the Sōzenji Yokouana Group were created by digging horizontally into the slopes of this valley. There are 15 confirmed rock-cut tombs arranged in an east-west direction. Over time, sediment filled the valley, leaving behind a 2-3 meter cliff, burying most of the tomb entrances except for a few. Excavations did not extend deeply into the buried valley, so additional undiscovered tombs may still exist <ref name=hokokusho8p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.8.</ref>.
To the southeast of the site lies the {{ill|Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins|ja|郡山遺跡|Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins|lt=Koriyama Site}}, believed to have housed the capital of [[Mutsu Province]] until 724. This coincides with the period when the rock-cut tombs were in use. On the opposite side of the Hirose River to the east is the {{ill|Minami-Koizumi Site|ja|南小泉遺跡}}, a settlement that has existed since the [[Kofun period]]. These settlements are considered potential residences of the tomb users.
== Overview of the Rock-Cut Tombs == The excavators numbered the 15 rock-cut tombs sequentially from west to east, with tomb 1 being closer to the mountain and tomb 15 closer to the river. The first tomb discovered in [[1953]] (Shōwa 28) was tomb 15. All tombs extend perpendicularly into the cliff, aligning with the natural contours of the terrain. At the western end, they face south, while at the eastern end, they face southeast. With the exception of tombs 7, 8, and 9, they are at approximately the same elevation.
Each tomb has a [[burial chamber]] (玄室), which is either square, [[rectangle|rectangular]], or [[trapezoidal]]. The floor of these chambers ranges from 1.4 to 2.75 meters in length. The chamber ceilings are either arched or resemble house roofs. The entrance (玄門) is narrower than the access tunnel ([[dromos|羨道]]). Notably, tomb 13 lacks a distinct entrance or tunnel, with a uniform width of 0.8 meters. Tombs 11 and 12 have adjacent burial chambers connected by a small hole. Tombs 1, 2, and 3 also share linked chambers, but this appears to be a later modification using a [[pickaxe]]-like tool.
Seven of the tombs feature raised platforms about 10 cm high, except for tomb 15, which has a prominent 50 cm-high platform. Eight tombs lack these platforms. Natural fractures in the rock allowed water to seep in, so some tombs include drainage channels. Human remains were found on the platforms in tombs 5 (one individual) and 9 (three individuals). Due to significant deterioration, only a few large bones, including skulls, were recovered. The remains in tomb 5 belonged to a middle-aged man, while those in tomb 9 included two middle-aged men and one young adult woman.
Artifacts found in seven tombs include 13 pieces of [[sue ware|須恵器]], 8 pieces of [[haji ware|土師器]], and a single iron [[knife|刀子]]. These artifacts were discovered in three locations: inside the burial chambers, outside the entrance closures, and in the forecourts. This suggests they served different purposes—burial goods placed with the deceased, offerings made after the funeral, and items prepared for later interments. The knife, about 15.6 cm long with a wooden handle, was found on the platform in tomb 5, near the human remains.
== Later History == Long after the tombs were abandoned, {{ill|Sōzenji (Sendai)|ja|宗禅寺 (仙台市)}} was founded slightly north of the site during the medieval period. The cliff where the tombs are located became part of the temple grounds, with both the top and base of the cliff serving as cemetery areas <ref name=hokokusho4p>‘Sendai City Negishimachi Sōzenji Yokouana Group Excavation Report’, p.4.</ref>. In tomb 4, a later grave was dug so close to the tomb ceiling that a 30 cm hole had already opened by the time of excavation <ref name=hokokusho8p></ref>.
The tombs were well known to locals and the temple, and at times, vagrants took shelter inside. At an unknown date, three tombs were connected internally, likely by residents expanding the space. During the early [[Shōwa period]], local residents sealed the tomb entrances with soil for safety reasons <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>.
In [[1953]], during construction of a road along the Hirose River near the {{ill|Miyazawa Bridge (Hirose River)|ja|宮沢橋 (広瀬川)}}, excavation work uncovered one of the tombs <ref name=hokokusho4p></ref>. In the [[1970s]], when {{ill|Hirose Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り}} was planned, the temple cemetery had to be relocated, and the cliff containing the tombs was included in the new cemetery site. From January 13 to February 15, [[1975]] (Shōwa 50), the Sendai City Board of Education and the Civil Engineering Department conducted a 300m² excavation, confirming and investigating 14 tombs. After the survey, parts of the site were destroyed for leveling, and the remaining tombs were buried. Today, the site lies beneath the Sōzenji cemetery, with no visible traces of the tombs.
